statement: Wealth.com markets itself as an AI-powered estate and tax planning platform.
source_excerpt: necessary to deliver an industry-leading AI-powered estate and tax planning solution.
scope_status_rationale: Platform capability — applies broadly; conservatively kept in scope.

statement: Ester is described as a proprietary, deterministic AI engine.
source_excerpt: At its core is Ester®—a proprietary, deterministic AI engine that transforms static legal documents and tax returns into actionable, visual intelligence
scope_status_rationale: Deterministic-calc layer + grounding rules are core to Slice 4.

statement: Platform claims SOC 2 Type II compliance with encryption at rest/transit.
source_excerpt: All layers sit atop enterprise-grade security (SOC 2 Type II, encryption at rest/transit) and compliance controls.
scope_status_rationale: Auth / security baseline required for Slice 1 (Auth+Tenant+Identity).

statement: Ester comprises specialized agents — Estate Planning Agent (all customers) and Tax Planning Agent (Tax Planning customers only).
source_excerpt: now evolved into a system of specialized agents (Estate Planning Agent available to all; Tax Planning Agent included for Tax Planning customers).
scope_status_rationale: Ester architecture / ML-ops detail required for Slice 4 extractor and ADR.

statement: Five strategic pillars: AI-Native Deterministic Outputs, Enterprise Security, Seamless Integrations, Advisor-Led Client Experience, Distribution Leverage.
source_excerpt: 5.2.1 AI-Native Design with Deterministic Outputs
scope_status_rationale: Extractor RAG/vector/chunking architecture required for Slice 4 extractor flow.

statement: Architecture organized in six logical layers: Presentation, AI/Intelligence, Data Services, Execution, Integration, Security/Observability.
source_excerpt: Presentation Layer: Advisor Workbench, Client Portal, and white-labeled partner interfaces (React/TypeScript single-page applications).
scope_status_rationale: Platform compliance / security baseline required for Slice 1 platform foundation.

statement: Frontend stack: React 18 + TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, Recharts; monorepo via Nx.
source_excerpt: Frontend: React 18 + TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, Recharts for visualizations. Monorepo managed with Nx for shared component libraries.
open_question: PRD-prescribed stack — verify against real wealth.com if needed
scope_status_rationale: Platform / extractor / audit / auth baseline required by Demo MVP slices.

statement: Backend Services use Node.js / TypeScript (NestJS framework).
source_excerpt: Backend Services: Node.js / TypeScript (NestJS framework) for most microservices due to strong TypeScript ecosystem and real-time capabilities.
scope_status_rationale: Platform / extractor / audit / auth baseline required by Demo MVP slices.

statement: AI services: Python 3.12 (FastAPI) for Ester orchestration, LangChain/LlamaIndex for RAG; models on AWS SageMaker or Bedrock.
source_excerpt: AI Services: Python 3.12 (FastAPI) for Ester orchestration, LangChain/LlamaIndex for RAG pipelines, and custom deterministic logic layers. Models hosted on AWS SageMaker or Bedrock with proprietary fine-tuning.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Database: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension; secondary Pinecone vector store; Amazon S3 for raw document storage; Redis 7 for caching.
source_excerpt:
PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension for relational data + semantic vector search.
Pinecone (or self-hosted alternative) as secondary vector store for high-scale embeddings.
Amazon S3 for raw document storage (with server-side encryption).
Redis 7 for caching, session state, and rate limiting.
scope_status_rationale: Auth / security baseline required for Slice 1 (Auth+Tenant+Identity).

statement: Core entities: EstateDocument, Beneficiary, Asset, Household, Entity/Trust, Person, PlanVersion, AuditEvent.
source_excerpt: EstateDocument (id, firm_id, client_id, document_type, raw_s3_key, structured_json, version, status, created_at, updated_at, audit_hash)
scope_status_rationale: Document vault / audit log baseline required for Slice 3.

statement: Performance SLA: Ester Q&A latency P95 < 2 seconds.
source_excerpt: Ester Q&A latency: P95 < 2 seconds (cached embeddings + optimized RAG).
scope_status_rationale: Platform / extractor / audit / auth baseline required by Demo MVP slices.

statement: Estate Scan ingestion target < 30 seconds for typical 20-page document.
source_excerpt: Estate Scan ingestion: < 30 seconds for typical 20-page document (OCR + structuring).
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Overall platform uptime SLA 99.95% with multi-region failover.
source_excerpt: Overall platform uptime: 99.95% SLA with multi-region failover.
scope_status_rationale: Platform compliance / security baseline required for Slice 1 platform foundation.

statement: Ester is described as a system of five specialized agents: Estate Planning, Tax Planning, Document Extraction, Visualization, Q&A.
source_excerpt: Ester operates as a coordinated system of five specialized agents that collaborate via an orchestration layer:
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Document Extraction Agent claims >98% accuracy on standard instruments.
source_excerpt: The Document Extraction Agent uses a hybrid OCR + vision-language model pipeline to achieve >98% accuracy on standard instruments and flags low-confidence sections for human review.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Ester maintains golden test set of 1,000+ documents with extraction accuracy ≥98%, hallucination rate <0.1%.
source_excerpt:
Wealth.com maintains a golden test set of 1,000+ real (anonymized) estate documents with human-annotated ground truth. Key KPIs:

Extraction accuracy: ≥98% on core fields (trust terms, beneficiaries, assets).
Hallucination rate: <0.1%.
open_question: Section 19 cites 1,500+ docs and <0.5% hallucination — reconcile
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Estate Scan extracts approximately 500 structured elements across 18 major categories.
source_excerpt: Estate Scan extracts approximately 500 structured elements across 18 major categories
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Per-field confidence score 0-100; fields below 85% confidence routed to human-in-the-loop queue.
source_excerpt: Every extracted field receives a per-field confidence score (0–100) computed from OCR certainty, LLM agreement across layers, and deterministic rule validation. Fields below 85% confidence are automatically routed to the human-in-the-loop queue.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Estate Scan accuracy KPIs: ≥95% field-level, ≥99% trust-level summary, <15% human review rate.
source_excerpt:
Field-level extraction accuracy: Target ≥95% on core fields (validated quarterly against golden test set).
Trust-level summary accuracy: ≥99% (executive summary and key provisions).
Human review rate for legacy documents: <15% of total volume (improving with model retraining).
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Estate Scan supports drag & drop, email-in (firm-specific @scan.wealth.com address), API, and batch upload (up to 500 docs).
source_excerpt:
Email-in: Secure, encrypted email attachments sent to a firm-specific @scan.wealth.com address.
Batch upload: CSV manifests or ZIP archives for bulk legacy portfolios (up to 500 documents per batch).
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Client Portal authentication: MFA (authenticator/SMS/hardware key), magic-link email, SSO via SAML/OIDC.
source_excerpt:
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) via authenticator app, SMS, or hardware key.
Magic-link email (one-time, time-limited).
Single Sign-On (SSO) via SAML/OIDC integration with the firm’s identity provider or partner CRM.
scope_status_rationale: Auth / security baseline required for Slice 1 (Auth+Tenant+Identity).

statement: Audit trail uses Amazon S3 Object Lock WORM storage with minimum 7-year retention (configurable to 10+ years).
source_excerpt: The immutable audit trail is implemented as a centralized, cryptographically sealed event store using Amazon S3 Object Lock (or equivalent enterprise WORM storage) in compliance mode with a minimum 7-year retention policy (configurable to 10+ years for certain firms).
scope_status_rationale: Document vault / audit log baseline required for Slice 3.

statement: PRD claims system has logged more than 12 million auditable events across live clients with zero integrity incidents (April 2026).
source_excerpt: As of April 2026, the system has logged more than 12 million auditable events across live clients with zero integrity incidents.
scope_status_rationale: Platform compliance baseline (audit/log/encryption/UPL guardrail) required for Demo MVP regulated-fintech archetype.

statement: Targeted regulations: SEC RIA Recordkeeping Rule 204-2 (5-year minimum), FINRA Rule 17a-4(f) WORM, state insurance/legal-advice boundaries.
source_excerpt: SEC RIA Recordkeeping Rule 204-2: All books and records (client agreements, trade confirmations, advertisements, and now AI-assisted estate/tax plans) are retained for a minimum of five years
scope_status_rationale: Document vault / audit log baseline required for Slice 3.

statement: Encryption at rest: AES-256 with AWS KMS-managed keys; per-document encryption keys; BYOK/CMK option for enterprise.
source_excerpt: At Rest: AES-256 encryption using AWS KMS-managed keys. Every document in the Vault receives a unique per-document encryption key. Enterprise customers can opt for Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) or Customer-Managed Keys (CMK)
scope_status_rationale: Auth / security baseline required for Slice 1 (Auth+Tenant+Identity).

statement: TLS 1.3 (with fallback to 1.2 where required by legacy partners) enforced end-to-end.
source_excerpt: In Transit: TLS 1.3 (with fallback to 1.2 where required by legacy partners) enforced end-to-end. HSTS and certificate pinning are mandatory.
scope_status_rationale: Auth / security baseline required for Slice 1 (Auth+Tenant+Identity).

statement: Certifications listed: SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, CCPA/CPRA, GDPR, HIPAA, GLBA, NIST CSF 2.0, NIST AI RMF 1.0.
source_excerpt:
ISO 27001: Full Information Security Management System (ISMS) certification.
CCPA / CPRA: California consumer privacy rights with automated data subject request fulfillment.
GDPR: Compliance for international family offices
scope_status_rationale: Platform compliance / security baseline required for Slice 1 platform foundation.

statement: Access control combines RBAC + ABAC with 40+ granular permission scopes.
source_excerpt: Over 40 granular permission scopes are defined (e.g., documents:read_draft, ai:generate_tax_scenario, vault:share_external).
scope_status_rationale: Auth / security baseline required for Slice 1 (Auth+Tenant+Identity).

statement: Breach response SLAs: detection within 15 minutes, containment within 1 hour, customer notification within 72 hours.
source_excerpt: P0 (critical) SLAs: detection within 15 minutes, containment within 1 hour, customer notification within 72 hours (or sooner where legally required).
scope_status_rationale: Platform / extractor / audit / auth baseline required by Demo MVP slices.

statement: PRD says Ester core consists of proprietary fine-tuned models trained on a 1.2 million document legal/tax corpus.
source_excerpt: The core consists of proprietary fine-tuned models trained exclusively on a 1.2 million document legal/tax corpus
scope_status_rationale: Ester architecture / ML-ops detail required for Slice 4 extractor and ADR.

statement: PRD claims fine-tuning uses LoRA adapters on a Llama-3.1-70B-class base.
source_excerpt: Fine-tuning uses LoRA adapters on a Llama-3.1-70B-class base with domain-specific instruction tuning
open_question: PRD-prescribed; not a verifiable wealth.com public statement
scope_status_rationale: LLM hosting choice is part of extractor architecture (will use mocks/local at MVP; ADR will document).

statement: PRD says selective frontier-model use occurs via private endpoints — Azure OpenAI GPT-4o and Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet via AWS Bedrock PrivateLink.
source_excerpt: Selective orchestration with frontier models occurs only via private, zero-data-retention endpoints (Azure OpenAI GPT-4o private instances and Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet via AWS Bedrock PrivateLink).
open_question: PRD-prescribed model names; not verified public claim
scope_status_rationale: LLM hosting choice is part of extractor architecture (will use mocks/local at MVP; ADR will document).

statement: Prompt caching reduces token spend by 40-60% on repetitive estate queries.
source_excerpt: Prompt caching (exact + semantic TTL-based) reduces token spend by 40–60% on repetitive estate queries.
scope_status_rationale: Ester architecture / ML-ops detail required for Slice 4 extractor and ADR.

statement: RAG embedding model: domain-fine-tuned BGE-large-en-v1.5 and E5-mistral-7b-instruct trained on 800k+ estate/tax passages.
source_excerpt: Embedding Model: Domain-fine-tuned BGE-large-en-v1.5 and E5-mistral-7b-instruct models (continued pre-training on 800k+ estate/tax passages). Average embedding dimension 1024; cosine similarity threshold tuned to 0.78 for retrieval.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor RAG/vector/chunking architecture required for Slice 4 extractor flow.

statement: Reranker uses Cohere rerank-english-v3 or in-house BGE-reranker; final top-12 chunks injected into prompt.
source_excerpt: Retrieval Ranking: Multi-stage pipeline: (1) BM25 lexical + dense vector retrieval (top-50), (2) cross-encoder reranker (Cohere rerank-english-v3 or in-house BGE-reranker), (3) final top-12 chunks injected into prompt with full provenance metadata.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor RAG/vector/chunking architecture required for Slice 4 extractor flow.

statement: Section 19 evaluation harness: 1,500+ golden documents; extraction F1 ≥0.97; hallucination rate <0.5%.
source_excerpt:
A continuously maintained golden dataset of 1,500+ real (anonymized) estate documents with human-annotated ground truth drives all model decisions. Key metrics tracked in the MLOps dashboard:

Extraction F1-score: ≥0.97 on core fields.
Summarization faithfulness: ≥0.98 (using G-Eval + human spot-checks).
Calculation determinism: 100% match with rule-engine output.
Hallucination rate: <0.5%
open_question: Section 8 said 1,000+ docs and <0.1% — contradicts Section 19; flag
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Canary release shadow: 5% traffic for 48 hours before full rollout.
source_excerpt: Canary releases: 5% traffic shadow for 48 hours before full rollout.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Design system uses Tailwind CSS + Radix UI; aesthetic: deep navy (#0A2540) and soft sage (#E8F0E8) accents.
source_excerpt: The design system employs a single, unified component library built on Tailwind CSS (utility-first) and Radix UI primitives for accessibility and composability.
scope_status_rationale: Design-system / UX heuristic informs Demo MVP advisor UI.

statement: Custom AI-Trust heuristics: Transparency of Intelligence, Control over AI, Emotional Safety, Consistency of Trust.
source_excerpt: Transparency of Intelligence – Users must always understand why the system recommends or visualizes something
scope_status_rationale: Design-system / UX heuristic informs Demo MVP advisor UI.

statement: Assumption: Foundation-model providers maintain zero-data-retention private endpoints (owner CTO).
source_excerpt: Foundation-model providers maintain zero-data-retention private endpoints (CTO).
scope_status_rationale: Ester extraction-flow architecture required for Slice 4.

statement: API documented at developer.wealth.com with OpenAPI 3.1; OAuth2 Bearer token or mTLS auth.
source_excerpt: The Wealth.com REST API (v1) is documented at developer.wealth.com with OpenAPI 3.1 specification. All endpoints require OAuth2 Bearer token or mTLS.

statement: Sample API endpoint: POST /v1/documents/scan (auth OAuth2, multipart file).
source_excerpt: POST /v1/documents/scan – Ingest and extract document. Auth: OAuth2. Body: multipart/form-data file. Response: { "documentId": "uuid", "status": "PROCESSING", "confidence": 96.4 }

statement: Numerical grounding: math results must match rule-engine output within 0.0001 tolerance.
source_excerpt: numerical grounding (math results must match rule-engine output within 0.0001 tolerance)
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Policy engine scans prompts and outputs in <50ms.
source_excerpt: The engine uses a decision tree + LLM classifier to scan prompts and outputs in <50ms.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Drift monitoring: weekly statistical tests on embedding distributions and output distributions.
source_excerpt: Drift monitoring: weekly statistical tests on embedding distributions and output distributions.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: AI red-team program: quarterly internal and external red-team exercises targeting prompt injection, jailbreaks, bias.
source_excerpt: AI red-team program: quarterly internal and external red-team exercises targeting prompt injection, jailbreaks, and bias.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Platform blocks output construed as practicing law/insurance sales without human attorney/CPA review; jurisdiction-specific guardrails.
source_excerpt: The platform blocks any output that could be construed as practicing law or insurance sales without human attorney/CPA review. Jurisdiction-specific guardrails prevent generation of documents in states where the firm lacks licensed counsel.
scope_status_rationale: Platform compliance / security baseline required for Slice 1 platform foundation.

statement: Sample extracted JSON snippet shows California revocable trust example with overallConfidence 96.4.
source_excerpt:
{
  "estateDocumentId": "uuid-1234",
  "documentType": "REVOCABLE_TRUST",
  "jurisdiction": "California"
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Memory scopes: per-household long-term (vector embeddings), plan-version context (immutable snapshots), session memory (Redis-backed).
source_excerpt:
Per-household long-term memory: Vector embeddings of all documents, plans, and interactions for a client household (stored in pgvector/Pinecone).
Plan-version context: Immutable snapshots of every PlanVersion for auditability.
Session memory: Short-term conversation history within the current advisor or client session (Redis-backed).
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: RAG chunking targets 200-600 tokens per chunk with metadata (page, clause type, jurisdiction).
source_excerpt: Chunking Strategy: Legal documents are chunked at clause/subsection level (200–600 tokens) with semantic boundaries preserved via sentence-transformer embeddings. Metadata (page number, clause type, jurisdiction) is attached to every chunk.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Vector store: primary pgvector (Postgres 16 with IVFFlat + HNSW indexes, sharded by firm_id); secondary Pinecone pod-based.
source_excerpt: Vector Store: Hybrid tiered system – primary pgvector (Postgres 16 with IVFFlat + HNSW indexes, sharded by firm_id) for low-latency, transactional queries; secondary Pinecone pod-based index for high-scale partner embeddings
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Audit log JSON includes promptHash, modelVersion, citations array, guardrailStatus, deterministicValidation flag.
source_excerpt:
"promptHash": "sha256:abc123...",
    "modelVersion": "ester-tax-v2.3.1",
    "citations": ["doc-uuid-789:clause-14", "tax-return-2025:schedule-D"],
    "guardrailStatus": "PASSED",
    "deterministicValidation": true
scope_status_rationale: Document vault / audit log baseline required for Slice 3.

statement: Section 7520 rate is a slider input in real-time projections.
source_excerpt: Interest rates and Section 7520 rates
scope_status_rationale: Section 7520 rate is required input for the GRAT scenario (Slice 6).

statement: Disclaimer language stamped on Ester outputs: 'This is not legal or tax advice; consult your licensed professional.'
source_excerpt: Ester outputs are stamped with clear disclaimers (“This is not legal or tax advice; consult your licensed professional”).

statement: AI prompt/response logging anonymized at ingestion (PII redacted) yet fully reconstructible for internal audits.
source_excerpt: AI prompt/response logging is anonymized at ingestion (PII redacted) yet fully reconstructible for internal audits via secure, role-based access.

statement: Firm-level governance: four-eyes review for irrevocable trusts; supervisor sign-off for documents exceeding $10M assets.
source_excerpt: Firms define custom approval chains (e.g., four-eyes review for irrevocable trusts, supervisor sign-off for any document exceeding $10M in assets, compliance queue for AI-generated tax scenarios).
scope_status_rationale: Platform compliance / security baseline required for Slice 1 platform foundation.

statement: Ester orchestration described as a Level 3 teammate capable of research, analysis, and client-ready deliverables.
source_excerpt: elevates Ester from a document reader to a Level 3 teammate capable of research, analysis, and client-ready deliverables within a single workflow.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: Q&A query latency <80ms for cached prompt-cache hits with zero additional LLM tokens consumed.
source_excerpt: Given a repeated household Q&A query within TTL, When the gateway processes, Then cached response is served in <80ms with zero additional LLM tokens consumed.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: US-9.1 acceptance: drag-and-drop PDF, ingestion + extraction + enrichment in <20 seconds with ≥98% confidence.
source_excerpt: Given a clean digital revocable trust PDF, When the advisor drags it into the Workbench, Then Estate Scan completes ingestion, extraction, and enrichment in <20 seconds with ≥98% confidence across all fields.
scope_status_rationale: Extractor architecture / deterministic-calc grounding; required for Slice 4 (Ester-substitute extractor with citation grounding).

statement: QSBS Section 1202: up to 100% exclusion of gain on qualifying stock held for 5+ years (PRD glossary).
source_excerpt: QSBS (Qualified Small Business Stock) – Section 1202: Up to 100% exclusion of gain on qualifying stock held for 5+ years.

statement: RMD: Mandatory withdrawals from retirement accounts beginning at age 73 (PRD glossary).
source_excerpt: RMD (Required Minimum Distribution): Mandatory withdrawals from retirement accounts beginning at age 73.
scope_status_rationale: Platform / extractor / audit / auth baseline required by Demo MVP slices.

statement: Open question: What is the long-term viability of foundation-model providers' zero-data-retention guarantees?
source_excerpt: What is the long-term viability of foundation-model providers’ zero-data-retention guarantees?
open_question: Long-term viability of foundation-model zero-data-retention guarantees

statement: GRAT remainder validation: LLM proposed value accepted only if recomputed within 0.0001 tolerance.
source_excerpt: Given an LLM-proposed GRAT remainder value of $2.34M, When the deterministic layer runs, Then the value is recomputed using annuity formula and accepted only if within 0.0001 tolerance.
scope_status_rationale: Platform / extractor / audit / auth baseline required by Demo MVP slices.
